connections.name: Steam Link VR
requirements: Steam Link app on headset (free on Horizon Store)

knownIssues.title: Face/eye tracking increases CPU overhead
knownIssues.description: The Quest Pro's face and eye tracking features send additional data through the Link connection, slightly increasing CPU overhead on the PC side.
knownIssues.workaround: If CPU-bound, disable face/eye tracking in Oculus settings to free up resources. Only matters for CPU-limited setups.

knownIssues.title: Open-design peripheral light leak
knownIssues.description: The Quest Pro's open design lets in ambient light, which can interfere with tracking in bright environments.
knownIssues.workaround: Use the included light blockers or play in a dimmer room for best tracking accuracy.

optimizationTips.tip: Use eye-tracked foveated rendering when available
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: Games that support it can use your Quest Pro's eye tracking to focus rendering power where you're actually looking, giving you better performance.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Enable Dynamic Foveated Rendering in supported OpenXR titles. The Quest Pro's eye tracking allows the runtime to reduce peripheral render resolution by up to 50%, freeing GPU resources. Set foveation level to High in Oculus Debug Tool for maximum performance gain.

optimizationTips.tip: Local dimming improves dark scenes in Link mode
optimizationTips.simpleExplanation: The Quest Pro has special backlight zones that make dark areas in VR look much deeper and richer.
optimizationTips.advancedExplanation: Enable local dimming in Meta Quest settings for improved contrast ratio in Link mode. The Quest Pro's 500+ dimming zones provide OLED-like black levels. Note: may introduce slight blooming around bright objects on dark backgrounds.
